objectives: Analyze performance characteristics of mx20022-runtime workspace
Identify critical bottlenecks in store, engine, context, channels
Classify findings as critical, recommended, or acceptable-for-alpha
constraints: Alpha stage - prioritize correctness over perf where needed
important_facts: SQLite store uses sqlx pool with connect_lazy, no pool_size config wired through
PRAGMA WAL set per-connection via OnceCell init - only runs once but PRAGMA is per-connection
query() method loads ALL rows then filters in Rust
update_transaction does read-modify-write without SQL transaction wrapping
Context uses Box<dyn Any> with Vec<ValueMutation> per key plus unbounded audit_log
Engine semaphore pattern is correct for concurrency limiting
HTTP channels now use axum (not raw TCP) - no manual buffer sizing
set_active_transactions uses absolute set(1)/set(0), not inc/dec - race condition
Audit log entries are persisted one-at-a-time in a loop (N+2 queries per tx)
risks_issues: query() full-table scan: will degrade as transaction volume grows
update_transaction TOCTOU: concurrent updates can lose writes
set_active_transactions gauge is wrong under concurrency
PRAGMA WAL only applied on first pool connection

iteration.summary: Completed full read of 8 source files covering store, engine, app, transaction_manager, context, HTTP channels, admin host, and config. Identified critical bottlenecks in query(), update_transaction race condition, metrics gauge misuse, and PRAGMA initialization. HTTP channels have been migrated to axum so buffer concerns are resolved.
next_actions: Implement fixes for critical items if requested
